content.problemStatement:
Utkarsh has a permutation PPP of {0,1,2,‚Ä¶,N‚àí1}\{0, 1, 2, \dots, N-1\}{0,1,2,‚Ä¶,N‚àí1}.

He wants to find the number of pairs (L,R)(L, R)(L,R) (1‚â§L<R‚â§N)(1 \leq L \lt R \leq N)(1‚â§L<R‚â§N) such that swapping the elements PLP_LPL‚Äã and PRP_RPR‚Äã does not change the MEX of any subarray.

Formally, find the number of pairs (L,R)(L, R)(L,R) (1‚â§L<R‚â§N)(1\leq L \lt R \leq N)(1‚â§L<R‚â§N) satisfying the following condition:


Let P‚Ä≤P'P‚Ä≤ be the permutation defined as follows:

Pi‚Ä≤=PiP'_i = P_iPi‚Ä≤‚Äã=Pi‚Äã for i‚àà{1,2,3,‚Ä¶,N}‚àñ{L,R}i \in \{1, 2, 3, \ldots, N\} \setminus \{L, R\}i‚àà{1,2,3,‚Ä¶,N}‚àñ{L,R}
PL‚Ä≤=PRP'_L = P_RPL‚Ä≤‚Äã=PR‚Äã
PR‚Ä≤=PLP'_R = P_LPR‚Ä≤‚Äã=PL‚Äã


mex({Pi,Pi+1,‚Ä¶,Pj})=mex({Pi‚Ä≤,Pi+1‚Ä≤,‚Ä¶,Pj‚Ä≤})mex(\{P_i, P_{i+1}, \ldots, P_j\}) = mex(\{P'_i, P'_{i+1}, \ldots, P'_j\})mex({Pi‚Äã,Pi+1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Pj‚Äã})=mex({Pi‚Ä≤‚Äã,Pi+1‚Ä≤‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Pj‚Ä≤‚Äã}) for every 1‚â§i‚â§j‚â§N1 \leq i \leq j \leq N1‚â§i‚â§j‚â§N
For example, let N=7N = 7N=7 and P=[2,5,1,0,3,6,4]P = [2, 5, 1, 0, 3, 6, 4]P=[2,5,1,0,3,6,4]. Then,


Swapping the pair (1,3)(1, 3)(1,3), i.e swapping P1=2P_1 = 2P1‚Äã=2 and P3=1P_3 = 1P3‚Äã=1 would give us the permutation P‚Ä≤=[1,5,2,0,3,6,4]P' = [1, 5, 2, 0, 3, 6, 4]P‚Ä≤=[1,5,2,0,3,6,4]. This changes the mex of the subarray [3,4][3, 4][3,4], because mex({P3,P4})=mex({1,0})=2mex(\{P_3, P_4\}) = mex(\{1, 0\}) = 2mex({P3‚Äã,P4‚Äã})=mex({1,0})=2 but mex({P3‚Ä≤,P4‚Ä≤})=mex({2,0})=1mex(\{P'_3, P'_4\}) = mex(\{2, 0\}) = 1mex({P3‚Ä≤‚Äã,P4‚Ä≤‚Äã})=mex({2,0})=1, so (1,3)(1, 3)(1,3) is not a valid swap.
Swapping the pair (4,7)(4, 7)(4,7) gives us the permutation P‚Ä≤=[2,5,1,4,3,6,0]P' = [2, 5, 1, 4, 3, 6, 0]P‚Ä≤=[2,5,1,4,3,6,0]. This changes the mex of the subarray [7,7][7, 7][7,7] ‚Äî mex({P7})=mex({4})=0mex(\{P_7\}) = mex(\{4\}) = 0mex({P7‚Äã})=mex({4})=0 and mex({P7‚Ä≤})=mex({0})=1mex(\{P'_7\}) = mex(\{0\}) = 1mex({P7‚Ä≤‚Äã})=mex({0})=1. So, (4,7)(4, 7)(4,7) is also not a valid pair.
However, it can be verified that the pair (2,6)(2, 6)(2,6) is valid ‚Äî swapping P2P_2P2‚Äã and P6P_6P6‚Äã gives us the permutation P‚Ä≤=[2,6,1,0,3,5,4]P' = [2, 6, 1, 0, 3, 5, 4]P‚Ä≤=[2,6,1,0,3,5,4], and mex(P[i‚Ä¶j])=mex(P‚Ä≤[i‚Ä¶j])mex(P[i \ldots j]) = mex(P'[i \ldots j])mex(P[i‚Ä¶j])=mex(P‚Ä≤[i‚Ä¶j]) for every 1‚â§i‚â§j‚â§71 \leq i \leq j \leq 71‚â§i‚â§j‚â§7.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain an integer TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Description of each test case:
The first line contains a single integer NNN, denoting the size of the permutation PPP.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers P1,P2,‚Ä¶,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1‚Äã,P2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,PN‚Äã, denoting the permutation PPP.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of pairs (L,R)(L,R)(L,R) such that swapping PLP_LPL‚Äã and PRP_RPR‚Äã does not change the MEX of any subarray.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105
It is guaranteed that PPP is a permutation of {0,1,2,‚Ä¶,N‚àí1}\{0, 1, 2, \dots, N-1\}{0,1,2,‚Ä¶,N‚àí1}.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There are no valid pairs.
Test case 222: There is only one valid pair, (L,R)=(2,6)(L,R) = (2,6)(L,R)=(2,6), i.e, swapping P2=5P_2 = 5P2‚Äã=5 and P6=6P_6 = 6P6‚Äã=6 to obtain the permutation [2,6,1,0,3,5,4][2, 6, 1, 0, 3, 5, 4][2,6,1,0,3,5,4].
